{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child mortality rate typically refers to the number of deaths of children under the age of 5 years. This is a widely accepted definition used by organizations such as the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF). While some sources might focus on different age ranges, the most comprehensive and commonly used range for child mortality rate is 0-5 years.", "answer_choice": "A"}
